what_it_does: Builds the live context and system prompt for the floating Vi assistant in Counterpart by reading project registries at call time; it returns a situational snapshot, a traceable system prompt assembled from tagged sections, registry-driven suggestions/streams/makeables, and a generation context that maps live tweaks to resolver preferences.
observations: The module owns no state and composes from global window registries such as MS_DATA, MS_CONTEXT, MS_MEMORY, MS_FEEDBACK, MS_DECISIONS, MS_ARTIFACTS, MS_STREAMS, MS_KINDS, and MS_IDENTITY.
It constructs Vi's system prompt as an ordered list of sections, each tagged with an identity aspect, so the same builder can feed both the runtime prompt and the Identity surface.
It includes an identityAspectBlock that gives Vi detailed, panel-specific awareness when the user is viewing her own identity console.
genContext maps live tweak values into resolver preferences and carries the project brief, bridging the committed look into generation.
suggestions and knowsList are read-only and rely on MS_DATA seeds and MS_MEMORY recall.
It uses defensive guards so missing registries degrade gracefully rather than crashing the page.
standouts: systemPrompt and the Identity surface share the same sections builder, making the live prompt inspectable.
identityAspectBlock is unusually self-referential: it tells Vi which identity panel the user is viewing and how to act on it.
genContext is the explicit bridge that prevents generation from defaulting to a blind default tier.
opts.preview lets the same composition code render the prompt without marking learnings as applied.
conventions: Wraps the namespace in an IIFE attached to window.MS_VI_BRAIN.
Uses camelCase function names and const lookup tables.
Guards every registry access with existence checks.
Prefers functional composition over mutable state.
Hardcodes classification tables for pages, zones, decisions, and voice styles.
concerns: identityAspectBlock references many action directive syntaxes that are not defined or validated in this file.
scopeForPage and ZONE_KINDS contain hardcoded page/zone lists that can drift as the app grows.
surfaceFacts falls back to D.mockups when MS_ARTIFACTS is missing, but D.mockups may not exist either.
genContext duplicates accent into both accent and base keys without explaining why.
knowsList concatenates memory content directly, which could become verbose.
notes: This file is the mind composer for Vi: it owns no state and derives every prompt from the same live registries the studio surfaces use.
questions: What is the exact contract for MS_IDENTITY methods such as facetForZone, activeBehaviours, get, facets, and behaviours?
How are the action directives like [[facet|...]], [[behaviour|...]], and [[dial|...]] parsed and executed elsewhere?
Does MS_FEEDBACK.learningsForScope differ from learningsForPrompt only in side effects, or also in filtering?
What is the expected shape of objects returned by MS_KINDS.get and MS_STREAMS.forSurface?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header states it composes live sources into a snapshot, system prompt, and registry-driven suggestions/streams/makeables, and the implementation does exactly that.
intention_signals: To make Vi feel like one continuous, project-aware mind whose reasoning is inspectable and editable by the user, while ensuring generated parts inherit the live committed look and learned preferences.
novelty: The system prompt is built as tagged sections that are also rendered on the Identity surface, creating a feedback loop where the user sees the literal prompt Vi runs on.
contribution: A stateless, centralized composer that turns live project registries into Vi's situational snapshot, traceable system prompt, and actionable in-context suggestions, keeping Vi's mind aligned with the studio's current state.
summary_for_embedding: vi-brain.js is a stateless composer that builds Vi's live situational snapshot, traceable system prompt, and in-context suggestions by reading project registries at call time.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to make Vi contextually aware and inspectable by deriving her mind from the same single-source registries the studio uses.

symbols.does: Selects a voice persona by style.
symbols.description: Looks up the requested style in the VOICE table and returns its persona string, defaulting to calm if the style is unknown.

symbols.does: Maps a page id to a feedback scope.
symbols.description: Classifies pages into synthesis, generation, or global scopes, mirroring the MEM_SCOPES vocabulary used by the feedback registry.

symbols.does: Lists producible kinds for the current zone.
symbols.description: Uses ZONE_KINDS to pick kind ids for the active zone, then resolves each through MS_KINDS.get and filters out missing entries.

symbols.does: Returns a human label for a workspace or page.
symbols.description: Prefers MS_DATA.pageMeta, then falls back to matching MS_DATA.workspaces, and finally returns the raw id or a generic label.

symbols.does: Returns a human label for a zone.
symbols.description: Resolves the zone label through MS_DATA.zoneMeta when that registry is available.
Combines project title, zone label, and page label into a structured 'where' object plus a human sentence describing the user's current location.
Reads the live tweaks via MS_DECISIONS for a fixed set of core decisions and returns the active option labels.
Prefers the project brief's jobToBeDone, goal, buildType, and tone, falling back to the project's subtitle.
Counts total, approved, and candidate artifacts from MS_ARTIFACTS and lists the three most recent entries.
Queries MS_MEMORY with a string built from location, project title, and brief, returning high-scoring memory items.

symbols.does: Lists streams available on the current surface.
symbols.description: Calls MS_STREAMS.forSurface for the current workspace page when the registry exists.

symbols.does: Builds resolver preferences from live tweaks.
symbols.description: Maps tweak values such as accent, radius, density, warmth, transFeel, and voiceStyle into a preferences object and attaches the project brief.

symbols.does: Adds identity-console context to the prompt.
symbols.description: When the user is viewing an identity panel, returns a detailed block naming the panel, explaining its meaning, and listing available action directives for that aspect.

symbols.does: Collects live facts about the current surface.
symbols.description: Returns surface-specific snippets such as candidate counts on review/generate pages, sequence progress on sequence/overview pages, and ops KPIs in the ops zone.
Assembles panel rows for aim, made artifacts, surface facts, memory, and applicable learnings, each with an icon and label.
Reads MS_DATA.viSuggestions for the current workspace page and returns up to three seeds.

symbols.does: Composes the system prompt as tagged sections.
symbols.description: Gathers context from registries and constructs an ordered list of prompt sections, each with an id, identity aspect, label, and body, so the same builder can feed both Vi and the Identity surface.
Pushes a section object into the output array only if its body is non-empty after trimming, ensuring no blank sections appear.

symbols.does: Joins sections into a single prompt string.
symbols.description: Maps the composed sections to their bodies and joins them with double newlines to produce the final system prompt.
